content: This mirrors exactly what I started doing after we talked at the MTOTA workshop. goal measure changes the whole conversation.
We are seeing this pattern in the pilot outcome data too. Readers who had goal measure administered at intake show higher satisfaction scores at week 8.
Carla - yes and I think it is not just the tool but the shift in who is setting the priorities. Worth a deeper look in the Q2 write-up.
Thanks for the honest feedback Frank. The friction you mentioned on the goal measure scoring flow is on the v3.1 backlog.
Using this with my trainer participants too. The graded motor imagery framing pairs really well with the 30-minute version.
Buy cheap followers now!!!
Jasmine has been great on the floor. Glad this is documented - really useful read for other students thinking about pain-focused fieldwork.
This finally made the distinction click for me. I've been defaulting to graded activity when half my workload actually needs exposure. Bookmarking this.
Glad it helped, Hannah. The tell for me is whether avoidance is driven by tissue tolerance or by fear - that's usually the fork in the road.
Explain Pain was the one that rewired me too. Adding Being Mortal to my list now - hadn't thought of it as an Occupational Therapy book but you're right.
The bit about keeping lived experience at the center of every spec is exactly the tension I felt joining the group. Well said.
Classic technique is so underrated for low-impact aerobic work. I send a lot of my home-health clients out on the flats once the snow sets in.
I used to punt sleep to the PCP too. Adding a short version of this to my initial evals starting Monday. Thank you for the script.
Jasmine, this hour-by-hour breakdown is gold for anyone deciding on a pain-focused placement. The reality of the documentation load is rarely shown.
The re-administration cadence is where I keep falling down. Do you set calendar reminders or build it into the visit template?
Greg - I build it into the visit template at the 4-week and discharge marks. A reminder I can ignore; a required field in the note I can't.
Love this reframe. A pothos is basically a forgiving habit tracker. Going to try it with a couple of clients rebuilding morning routines.
Increase your blog traffic 10x guaranteed click here!!!
Return-to-run logs from a coach are weirdly reassuring. Nice to see graded loading applied to your own ankle, not just a handout.
Great perspective, Rowan. The translation problem between clinic needs and product specs is the whole game - glad it's getting written about.
